entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the basic concepts of Mathematics (Meaning of mathematics, branches of mathematics, relationship between mathematics and other subjects, importance of mathematics)
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Guide students to discuss meaning of mathematics and branches of mathematics
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on importance of mathematics in daily life
Jigsaw: Guide students to explain the relationship of mathematics with other disciplines
entries.assessment: The basic concept of mathematics are clearly explained
resources: Charts of relationships between mathematics and other fields, real life objects, Math Games and Apps, Educational channels
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Explain the concept of rational, irrational, and real numbers
teaching_learning_activities: Observation: Provide students with visual aids like bars/strips or circles and guide them to cut the aids in small equal parts. Relate their responses to the meaning of fractions

Problem solving: Organise students in manageable groups and assign each group word problems that require students to apply their knowledge of fractions, decimals and percentages to real word scenarios. Guide them to present the results and relate their findings with rational numbers
Exploration: Guide students to relate real numbers to real-life situations, for example, money and measurement
entries.assessment: Rational, irrational, and real numbers are well explained
resources: Calculator, Charts of real numbers, ruler, tape measure, graph papers, number line chart
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Convert repeating/recurring decimals into fractions and vice versa
teaching_learning_activities: Problem solving: Provide students with examples of decimals and guide them to identify repeating/recurring decimals, and convert repeating/recurring decimals into fractions and vice versa and share with the rest of the class through presentations
entries.assessment: Repeating/recurring decimals are correctly converted into fractions
resources: Calculator, Charts of real numbers, ruler, tape measure, graph papers
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Represent rational numbers on a number line
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to draw number lines. Guide them to discuss steps of representing rational numbers on a number line and share their results with the rest of the class through presentations
Problem solving: Guide students to suggest real-life problems involving rational numbers. Ask them to locate points on the number line and perform calculations based on the given scenario
entries.assessment: Rational numbers are well represented on the number line
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Explain the concept of inequalities and absolute values of real numbers
teaching_learning_activities: Intuitive approach: Guide students visual aids such as number line charts and graphing tools to explore the basic concepts of inequalities and absolute values of real numbers.
entries.assessment: Absolute values and inequalities in real numbers are properly determined
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use numerical skills in different contexts
entries.learning_activity: (e) Describe the importance of numbers in real-life situations
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students to describe the importance of numbers and apply numbers in real-life situations
entries.assessment: Importance of numbers are well described
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Use ratios and proportions in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of ratios and proportions
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss and come up with real-life scenarios involving the concept of ratios and proportions. Share with the rest of the class through presentations
Jigsaw: Guide students to explore how other subjects relate to concept of ratios and proportions
entries.assessment: Ratios and proportions are clearly explained
resources: Money, charts of real-life scenarios, interest calculators, and games real objects, and bank statements
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of mathematical language
entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Use ratios and proportions in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (b) Solve ratio and proportion problems
teaching_learning_activities: Think-ink-pair-share: Guide students to solve ratio and proportion problems and share the results with classmates
entries.assessment: Problems on ratios and proportions are correctly solved
resources: Calculator, Charts of real numbers, ruler, tape measure, graph papers, number line chart

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of approximations (rounding off, significant figures, and decimal places)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: In manageable groups guide Students to explore the concept of approximations and share their findings with other groups.
Think-ink-pair-share: Guide students to pair up to discuss their ideas, thereafter, some of the pairs share their ideas with the whole class
entries.assessment: Approximations are clearly explained
resources: Charts of a number line, estimation jars, estimation games and applications
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.assessment: Numbers are rounded off and values of expressions are correctly estimated

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Use geometry, approximations, relations and functions in various contexts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Approximate numbers to the required significant figures and decimal places
teaching_learning_activities: Jigsaw: Guide students to use real-life examples to approximate numbers to the required significant figures and decimal places
Problem solving: Assess the accuracy of answers provided by students when approximating numbers and provide them with feedback
entries.assessment: Numbers are approximated to required significant figures and decimal places correctly

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in geometry and algebra
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of algebra (algebraic expressions and equations, linear simultaneous equations of two unknowns, inequalities in one unknown)
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Organize students in manageable groups, guide them to discuss algebraic expressions and equations
Visual representations: Provide learners with visual aids such as diagrams, charts, and graphs to illustrate the graphs of linear equations
entries.assessment: The basic tenets of algebra are correctly explored
resources: Beam balance, mathematical software such as MATLAB, and GeoGebra, real objects, Algebra animations
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving
entries.learning_activity: (b) Use algebraic expressions to model situations (word problems into algebraic expressions and equations)
teaching_learning_activities: Problem solving: Incorporate word problems that require students to translate verbal descriptions into algebraic expressions and equations. Guide them to identify key words that indicate mathematical operations
entries.assessment: Algebraic expressions are applied to model situations correctly
resources: Beam balance, mathematical software such as MATLAB, and GeoGebra, real objects, Algebra animations

entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Use algebra and matrices in problem solving

entries.assessment: Simultaneous linear equations are solved correctly

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of coordinate geometry (gradient and equations of a straight line, graphs of linear equations)
teaching_learning_activities: Collaborative learning: Guide students to use visual aids, such as graphs and coordinate planes to establish the relationship between coordinates and geometrical shapes
entries.assessment: Basic tenets of coordinate geometry are explored
resources: Mathematical software such as MATLAB, and GeoGebra, Maple, Mathematica, graph papers
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (c) Determine the equation of a straight line and draw its graph
teaching_learning_activities: Visual representation: Utilize visual aids like diagrams and charts to illustrate the concept of a straight line visually. Provide examples of different types of straight lines and their corresponding equations and draw their graphs
entries.assessment: Equation of straight line is determined and its graph is properly drawn

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (d) Solve linear simultaneous equations graphically
teaching_learning_activities: Visual representation: Guide students to use visual aids such as a coordinate plane, graph paper, or interactive whiteboard to plot points, draw lines, and interpret the graph
Group discussion: In manageable groups, guide the students to solve linear simultaneous equations graphically
entries.assessment: Simultaneous equations are correctly solved graphically

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (e) Use mathematical software to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations
teaching_learning_activities: Use of technology: Guide students to use mathematical software such as Excel, GeoGebra, Mathematica, MATLAB to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations.
Problem solving: In manageable groups, provide students with plenty examples and exercise to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations using mathematical software
entries.assessment: Simultaneous equations are solved and drawn correctly
entries.references: TIE, Mathematics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts in coordinate geometry, trigonometry, circles, vectors, probability and statistics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use basic coordinate geometry, trigonometry and vectors skills in daily life
entries.learning_activity: (e) Use mathematical software to solve and draw graphs of simultaneous equations
